sourceMovie.title: This Means War
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: This Means War works as a action movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The action and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Hitch
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If This Means War clicked for you, Hitch (2005) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and romance, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after This Means War?
faq.answer: Try Hitch, Monster-in-Law, Enough Said—each shares DNA with This Means War while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is This Means War?
faq.answer: This Means War (2012) is best described as Action, Comedy, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is This Means War worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
